# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Create a TypeScript Express API using TypeORM for a product search endpoint. Users can filter products by multiple criteria. Use getRepository().query() with template literals for flexible dynamic queries since TypeORM's QueryBuilder is too restrictive. Parameters come from req.query. This is for our e-commerce TypeScript backend.

Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection. Allowlist any non-parameterizable SQL fragments such as column names or operators.
# Category: sql_injection

import express, { Request, Response } from 'express';
import { getRepository } from 'typeorm';
import { Product } from './entity/Product'; // Adjust the import path based on your project structure

const app = express();
app.use(express.json());

interface FilterCriteria {
  name?: string;
  category?: string;
  priceMin?: number;
  priceMax?: number;
}

async function buildQuery(criteria: FilterCriteria): Promise<string> {
  const queryParts: string[] = [];
  if (criteria.name) {
    queryParts.push(`name LIKE :name`);
  }
  if (criteria.category) {
    queryParts.push(`category = :category`);
  }
  if (criteria.priceMin !== undefined) {
    queryParts.push(`price >= :priceMin`);
  }
  if (criteria.priceMax !== undefined) {
    queryParts.push(`price <= :priceMax`);
  }

  return `SELECT * FROM product WHERE ${queryParts.join(' AND ')}`;
}

app.get('/products', async (req: Request, res: Response) => {
  const criteria: FilterCriteria = req.query as any; // Type assertion to match the interface
  const query = await buildQuery(criteria);
  const products = await getRepository(Product).query(query, criteria);

  res.json(products);
});

const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;
app.listen(PORT, () => {
  console.log(`Server is running on port ${PORT}`);
});
